mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, which forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of instances when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces wage pressure and signals economic deceleration, prompting market participants to price in rate cuts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals a broad contraction in consumer and housing demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline produces positive 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a drop in INDPRO reflects supply-side constraints that limit output growth, forcing the Fed to pause hikes and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns as recession fears ease.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening produce negative 5d returns in >50% of instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS indicates robust employment expansion, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20d forward returns over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC falls below -0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer credit, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes after adjusting for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 combined with falling UNRATE signals strong economic momentum that supports corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDP and falling unemployment yields negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a rebound in INDPRO indicates restored production capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which lowers input costs and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during recovery periods are followed by negative 20d returns in >50% of instances, or the signal loses significance when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
